pages.raw_text:
Most of the subcontracts are now
let.


Contractor George S. Deits is
building a brick addition to the
Northern Pacific depot, which will
probably be completed early in
February. It's dimensions are 50x40
feet. The lunch counter will be
removed from it's present location
in the waiting room and fitted up
in the new building.


The statement is made by coal
dealers that the Southern Pacific
has decided to change from oil to
coal on all of it's coast branches
now using oil. Orders for 10000
tons month have been placed for
Carbonate coal. 1t is said that oil
burns and warps the locomotive
boilers so that they leak.


The Baltimore & Ohio has COM
pleted the installation of block sig
nails on the main line between Bal
timore and Pittsburg. The signal
stations are located at uniform
distance of 1000 feet apart in the
thickly settled districts. This will
greatly facilitate the movement of
traffic. as the company will not
only be able to handle more trains,
but will be able to maintain faster
sch edples and make better time.


Portland, Ore - The Northern
Pacific will establish a grain port
at some point on the north bank
of the Columbia in opposition to
Astoria. The Oregon Railway &
Navigation company will run its
grain trains to the mouth of the
Columbia on the south bank and
sound cities will lose the export
grain trade.


The wheat trains will be run
through Portland without stopping
Common point freight rates will be
extended to the new ports.


The Northern Pacific officials
have not kept their intentions Sc
cret and engineers have for some
time been engaged in making ex
tensions of the system to the lower
Columbia.


Local railway men are much in
terested in the general movement
for an increase in wages among
western railway employees.


Indications are that this move
ment is rapidly approaching a cri
sis, as representatives of four OF
the largest employees organizations
are in conference over the subject
in Chicago.


The organizations represented
are: brotherhood of Railroad
Trainmen, with a membership Of
go 900; the Brotherhood of Locomo
tive Firemen, with membership o.
$4,000: the Brotherhood of Locomo
tive Engineers, with a membership
of Al ,000 and the Order of Railway
Conductors, with membership O
$4,000.


1t is understood that an increase
of II per cent has been decided
upon by members of the committee
THE employees of the roads which
have voluntarily advanced thu
wages of their employees are no
represented. The employees repre-
lsented are mainly from those roads
(which have their terminals in ST
Paul, Minneapolis, st. Louis, Kan
sas City, Indianapolis and Omaha
Among the roads thus intereste'
include the Northern Pacific, Grea
Northern. Chicago, Milwaukee 4
ST. Paul, the Chicago Il Northwest
ern, Wisconsin Central. lllinoi
Central and several others.


IL the railroads do not make tn
Required advances, a convention
representing 100000 men will b
called for January in st. Loui
and general federation OF all raj
iway employees formed.
